method: per-cell control twin (channel omitted); no-op = rendered==control != intended. Probe forms: style-lab-{flex-split,surface,inputs,controls}-claude.
crossCuttingRules: Component VERSION must match the live framework or the whole desktop style block silently no-ops (numberField v3=no-op vs v5=works). Always copy versions from the running app.
A flex container MUST set display:'flex' or flexDirection/gap are inert.
Size flex-split children via desktop.dimensions.width (reaches outer div); customStyle:{flex} is inert on the outer div.
font on a CONTAINER is a no-op — put font on the text child.

MEASUREMENT: in LIVE mode, text/refListStatus components do NOT carry the data-sha-c-name attribute (only containers do). Query by component name only finds containers — read a CONTAINER's innerText to verify the text inside it. (Edit mode does stamp the attr on text, which is misleading.)

MEASUREMENT: a dispatched DOM MouseEvent does NOT fire a React/antd component's onChange (e.g. ant-select selection). Use a REAL click (Playwright browser_click) to trigger onChange handlers, or your interaction silently no-ops.

desktop block on the card; proven in the live form's rail panels (v3)
via dataContext + list style channels; proven in live form (v11/7)
use `hidden` code-mode {_mode:'code',_code:'return <hide?>'} — NOT customStyle visibility
rows.fix: customVisibility is a NO-OP on row-template components (proven: empty chips/badges stayed visible); `hidden` hides correctly. Hide-when-empty: hidden returns true when the bound array/value is empty.

rows.key: the LEGACY `style` JS-string ('return {overflow:\'visible\'}') renders INLINE and reaches the .sha-components-container-inner div — the ONLY channel that overrides the framework's hard-coded inner overflow:auto. VERIFIED 2026-06-30 live: setting it makes the inner's computed overflow match the value (no ▲▼ scroll arrows). Use overflow:'visible' on inner card rows, overflow:'hidden' on the card root.
rows.fix: THIS is the datalist-card scroll-arrow fix — NOT a global CSS override, NOT htmlRender. The `overflow` boolean prop and `customStyle` do NOT do this; the `style` channel does.

rows.key: multi-line body text in a datalist row card: style 'return {whiteSpace:\'normal\',wordBreak:\'break-word\',overflow:\'hidden\',display:\'block\',maxWidth:\'100%\'}' + dimensions.minHeight '24px' (reserve) → text wraps to full height, no form-item collapse, no overlap with the meta row below.
rows.fix: the form-item wrappers still report a short height, but with overflow:visible on the parent rows + the minHeight reserve that is harmless — content paints fully and siblings don't overlap.

rows.key: in a row card: style 'return {display:\'inline-flex\',alignItems:\'center\',height:\'26px\',overflow:\'visible\',whiteSpace:\'nowrap\'}' + solidBackground:true → uniform, vertically-centred pill that doesn't collapse.

RESOLVED 2026-06-30 (was wrongly recorded as a hard limit needing global CSS). Root cause: the framework forces min-height:0 on each form-item-control-input (atomic class), so multi-line content paints via overflow:visible out of a collapsed wrapper → overlap + the container inner overflow:auto shows scroll arrows. FIX is markup-only: (1) `style` overflow on every container (visible on rows, hidden on card root); (2) dimensions.minHeight '24px' + style whiteSpace:normal/wordBreak on the body text; (3) dimensions.minHeight:'fit-content' on containers; (4) refListStatus pills styled inline-flex height 26px; (5) header flex row justifyContent:'space-between' for badges-left/actions-right.
rows.fix: canonical live examples: view-requirement-card, view-endpoint-row, view-role-row (rows of view-definition-details-claude). See component-recipes.md → 'Datalist row-template card' and the requirement-datalist-row block + overlay.

rows.key: dataContext.permanentFilter = {and:[{'==':[{var:'<fk>.id'},{evaluate:[{expression:'{{data.id}}',required:false,type:'mustache'}]}]}]}. `required:false` DROPS the clause when the value is empty (show-all-if-none). Mustache path for the form record is {{data.id}}; field path is the camelCase FK + '.id' (backend-verify it). Proven 2026-06-30.

rows.channel: filter by a GLOBAL appContext/globalState value
rows.fix: CONFIG-ONLY DOES NOT WORK (proven 2026-06-30, BOTH appContext and globalState). Two walls: (1) an autocomplete's onChangeCustom in the global `header` form does NOT fire on selection — the global value is never written; (2) even when present, the datatable permanentFilter evaluates {{contexts.appContext.X}} / {{globalState.X}} as EMPTY at query time and tables do NOT refetch on a global change. A global cross-screen filter needs FRONTEND-SOURCE app wiring (app-provider seed from storage before render + a dispatching selector + reactive refetch). NOTE: filtering by the form's OWN record (data.id) works fine (row above) — only GLOBAL/session values fail. See memory reference-appcontext-project-filter.

rows.key: Compute counts in formSettings.onAfterDataLoad: `var r = await http.get('/api/dynamic/<Mod>/<Entity>/Crud/GetAll?filter='+<parentFk filter>+'&properties=id&maxResultCount=1000'); form.setFieldsValue({xCount: (r.data.result.items||[]).length});` then bind a text/badge content to `data.xCount`. Use items.length, NOT result.totalCount (unreliable at small maxResultCount). The reliable way to show a live collection count in a header/badge (the live appContext/context read in a text is NOT — see the global-filter row). Proven on the rail count badges + the View Requirements count.
